meta.title: Unit 3 — Pronouns & I/me (Test 6)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Identify pronoun errors and replace nouns with the best pronoun.

questions.question: Which pronoun replaces a male person?
questions.explanation: "he" is used to replace a male person's name.

questions.question: In the sentence "I call Rosa," 'I' is the subject.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'I' comes before the verb 'call' — it's the subject of the sentence.

questions.question: Rosa calls ___. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "me" comes after the verb (object). Rosa calls me.

questions.question: "We" is a plural pronoun.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'we' = the speaker + others — it's a plural pronoun.

questions.question: Do you know that a camel can eat a cactus? → Do you know that ___ can eat a cactus?
questions.explanation: "a camel" is an animal → replace it with "it".

questions.question: Fill in: ___ call Rosa. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "I" is the subject — it goes before the verb. I call Rosa.

questions.question: Mina is surprised! → ___ is surprised!
questions.explanation: Mina is female — we use 'She' to replace Mina's name.

questions.question: Sort these: subject pronoun or object pronoun?
questions.explanation: I/he/she/we = subject pronouns. me = object pronoun (comes after verbs).

questions.question: In the sentence "Do you all want some juice?," 'you' here means more than one person.
questions.explanation: Yes! When we say 'you all,' 'you' is used as a plural pronoun meaning multiple people.

questions.question: My sister is kind. → ___ is kind. (one word)
questions.explanation: My sister is female — we use 'She' to replace 'My sister'.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "I call Rosa." 'I' is the subject before the verb.

questions.question: Wolves don't eat people, so ___ are not in danger.
questions.explanation: 'we' = the speaker and others, showing that people (us) are not in danger.

questions.question: The sentence "Me am Oliver the otter" is correct.
questions.explanation: 'Me' cannot be the subject. Correct: "I am Oliver the otter."

questions.question: which pronoun is used in this sentence? "Do you go to school by bus?"
questions.explanation: "you" is the pronoun — it refers to the person being spoken to.

questions.question: Fill in: Rosa gives ___ the basket. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "me" is the object — it goes after the verb. Rosa gives me the basket.

questions.question: Match the noun phrase with the correct pronoun:
questions.explanation: He = male. They = plural. It = thing/animal.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'I' correctly?
options: I am not scared of the lion.
questions.explanation: "I am not scared" — 'I' is the subject before the verb 'am'. The others need 'me' (after verb/preposition).

questions.question: In the sentence "When do they come out at night?," 'they' correctly replaces 'wolves'.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'wolves' is plural — 'they' is the correct replacement.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "The lion roars at me." 'me' after the preposition 'at'.

questions.question: Which sentence has a mistake?
options: I am not scared of the lion.
questions.explanation: "sees I" is wrong — after a verb, use 'me': "A lion sees me."
